Gypsi Consulting — Prior Art & Public Record

The Mars Clock Drift Timeline

A Timestamped Prediction and Its Verifiable Record
Donald Paul Smith
ORCID: 0009-0003-7925-1653
Gainesville, Florida, USA
June 2026
DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18052963 arXiv: 2507.21388

1Purpose of This Document

This document records an independent research timeline: the development of the SDKP (Size × Density × Kinetics × Position) framework by Donald Paul Smith, a specific numerical prediction made with that framework regarding Mars relativistic clock drift, and the public, timestamped record of that prediction relative to later published research.

This document makes a narrow, checkable claim. It does not claim institutional endorsement, peer review, or formal advisory status with any agency. It is written so that a reader — a journalist, a scientist, a documentary researcher, or a member of the public — can verify each element independently.

2The Mars Clock Drift Timeline

2.1  The Prediction

In May 2025, Donald Paul Smith published a timestamped prediction for Mars relativistic clock drift using the SDKP/EOS framework, archived publicly at:

DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18052963

Predicted value: 477.14 microseconds per day

2.2  The Subsequent Publication

In July 2025, Ashby & Patla published a paper (arXiv:2507.21388) independently calculating Mars relativistic clock drift, arriving at a closely comparable figure.

Verifiable Fact The gap between these two timestamps and the closeness of the two numbers is the verifiable fact. Readers and reviewers are encouraged to check both timestamps and both calculations directly rather than rely on summary framing.

2.3  What This Does and Does Not Establish

This does NOT establishThis DOES establish
That NASA or NIST used, reviewed, or relied on this prediction That a timestamped public prediction exists predating the NIST-affiliated paper
That Donald Paul Smith acted as an advisor to NASA or NIST That FOIA and complaint records exist showing attempts to bring this to agencies’ attention
That the SDKP framework has been validated by independent physicists That the specific numerical outputs can be independently recalculated and checked
Any claim of plagiarism or wrongdoing by Ashby & Patla That two independent calculations arrived at similar values via different methods — which may simply reflect that both are constrained by the same underlying physics

2.4  The Lunar Offset

Separately, the framework includes a proposed explanation (the “Amiyah Rose Smith Law”) for a ~56 microsecond/day discrepancy sometimes discussed in lunar timekeeping contexts. This is presented as a hypothesis within the framework, not as an established correction adopted by any timing standard.

3Correspondence and Public Records

The following are real, filed records. They document attempts to bring the above timeline to the attention of relevant agencies — they are evidence of outreach, not of a response, endorsement, or advisory relationship.

RecordReference
NIST FOIA RequestDOC-NIST-2026-000433
NASA FOIA Request26-00541-F-HQ
NASA OIG Complaint#2026-030
GAO Complaints#COMP-26-002732, #COMP-26-002846
Senator Rick Scott Office (constituent case)IMA00334153

Anyone is free to file their own FOIA request referencing the above case numbers to independently verify their status and content.

4Note for Documentary Production

Editorial Guidance The strongest version of this story does not require the SDKP framework to be “correct” in a general sense. It only requires the timestamps and the two numbers to be real and checkable — which they are.

Claims beyond that — a “unified framework,” “theory of everything,” consciousness mapping, or similar — should be presented as Donald Paul Smith’s broader theoretical project and clearly separated from the narrow, verifiable Mars timeline claim documented here.

5The Broader SDKP Framework

Separately from the Mars timeline above, Donald Paul Smith has developed a broader theoretical framework (SDKP, SD&N, SDVR, and related components) archived across Zenodo and OSF. This is presented as an independent researcher’s ongoing theoretical work — it has not undergone formal peer review, and readers should evaluate it accordingly.

6Licensing and Attribution

The written documents, code, and figures referenced in this record and linked archives are original works by Donald Paul Smith and are released under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC BY 4.0) unless otherwise noted. Attribution is requested for reuse of these specific documents and code.

Scope of Licensing This licensing applies to the documents themselves — the specific text, figures, code, and presentation. It does not and cannot extend to underlying physical constants, mathematical relationships, or laws of nature, which are not subject to ownership.
